rows.description: Soluta Varia
rows.notes: Clear, centered page title at top of sheet. No monetary figures present; heading legible and distinct from entries.

rows.description: A Half years Poors Tax due at Lacy for Gardington
rows.notes: Amount clearly aligned in the rightmost three ruled columns as 0 | 16 | 0 (pounds | shillings | pence). Text is legible; column boundaries unambiguous.

rows.description: Pd the Cook for Sewell at Lacy
rows.notes: Numbers read clearly as 11 | 10 | 0, well aligned under the pounds/shillings/pence rulings. Description text is clear.

rows.description: Pd Mr Abwell Sr Will: Peters extraordinary Stipend for pursuing Studies in a foreign University for a year & half
rows.notes: Amount reads clearly as 10 | 0 | 0. Handwriting in description is legible though long; 'Abwell'/'Sr Will: Peters' reading is certain from script context.

rows.description: Pd Mr Wrights Bill for mending Coll: Plates from Nov: 14 1728 to May 8. 1730
rows.notes: Numeric grouping clearly 1 | 17 | 6. Dates in description are legible; alignment to columns is unambiguous.

rows.description: Pd Fees to Counsellor Merwin for examining the Title of Kings Nymmet Liberty &c
rows.notes: Amount 2 | 2 | 0 is clear. Surname of counsellor reads as 'Merwin' but could be alternate spelling (Merwin/Mervin); 'Kings Nymmet' interpreted from local place-name style—description legible but with minor orthographic uncertainty.

rows.description: Pd for carrying away rubbish
rows.notes: Numbers read 5 | 17 | 6 and align well. Short description is clear and unambiguous.

rows.description: Pd Mr Collison for Work and Timber
rows.notes: Large round figure 20 | 0 | 0 clearly written and aligned. Surname 'Collison' read from handwriting with good clarity.

rows.description: Pd Mr Pain the Brazier his Bill for the Kitchen Hall, Lodge & Chappel
rows.notes: Amount 15 | 12 | 6 is legible. 'Chappel' spelled archaically; 'Pain the Brazier' reading is clear though small.

rows.description: The Poors Tax for Yardington at Mids y
rows.notes: Amount reads 0 | 6 | 0. Place-name reads as 'Yardington' (or 'Gardington' variant in earlier line); 'Mids y' short for 'Midsummer' is inferred from abbreviation. Slight uncertainty in the place spelling.

rows.description: Pd Mr Townsend in part for Masons Work on the Back Buildings
rows.notes: Amount 52 | 10 | 0 is visually clear and aligned. Description is legible; 'Townsend' and 'Back Buildings' unambiguous.

rows.description: Pd the Cook for Sewell for Mids y
rows.notes: Amount 11 | 10 | 0 clearly written. This appears to be a second payment to the cook related to 'Mids y' (Midsummer).

rows.description: Pd Mr Trimble for leathering the kneeling stools &c in the Chappel
rows.notes: Amount 11 | 5 | 0 aligned and readable. Surname read as 'Trimble' (handwriting slightly compressed); 'leathering the kneeling stools' phrase deciphered from context but with some letter-level uncertainty.

rows.description: Postage of Letters on College Business
rows.notes: Amount reads 0 | 6 | 8; the '8' pence character is clear and within valid range. Description is straightforward and legible.

rows.description: Attree to Counsel for opinion and Gardy Titles
rows.notes: Amount 1 | 2 | 0 is readable but the description is uncertain: first word reads 'Attree' or 'At tree' and the tail phrase 'Gardy Titles' is a best interpretation of cramped handwriting. Significant uncertainty in exact wording of the description.

rows.description: Pd the Tavern Bill for Oct: & Nov: 12 last
rows.notes: Amount 1 | 8 | 0 clear. Date phrase 'Oct: & Nov: 12 last' legible; description unambiguous.

rows.description: Pd the gardener for toil: work done in the Back Court in Dr Holes time
rows.notes: Amount 1 | 11 | 4 readable though the pence digit is compact. 'Dr Holes' surname read from script but could be 'Dr Hales' or similar; chosen based on letter shapes and context.

rows.description: Pd Mr Gaxler for Work & materials on the Back Buildings
rows.notes: Amount 21 | 0 | 0 is clear. The surname appears as 'Gaxler' / 'Gazler' / possibly 'Eagler' in the handwriting; the reading is uncertain due to flourished letters, so name transcription is approximate.

rows.description: A Poors Tax for Yardington
rows.notes: Amount 0 | 6 | 0 clear. 'Yardington' matches prior occurrences though spelling varies; entry is concise and readable.

rows.description: Pd for painting the Ceilings in the Chappel
rows.notes: Large round number 45 | 0 | 0 clearly written and aligned. Description legible; painting of 'Ceilings' unambiguous.

rows.description: Pd for the last years Progress
rows.notes: Amount 3 | 17 | 0 readable. The description's final word is unclear in the handwriting (reads like 'Progress' or 'Progers'); chosen 'Progress' as the closest sense, but significant uncertainty remains.

rows.description: Pd Mr Owens for Candles for the Chappel to Octr. 15
rows.notes: Amount 6 | 10 | 0 clear. Surname read as 'Owens' (could be 'Owen' or 'Gones' depending on initial stroke); 'to Octr. 15' date is legible.

rows.description: Pd for Candles for the Buttry
rows.notes: Amount 4 | 7 | 9 clearly aligned. 'Buttry' (buttery) spelling visible in manuscript; description and numbers are legible.

rows.description: Pd for Candles for the Lodge
rows.notes: Amount 1 | 2 | 9 clearly written. Short description is unambiguous.

rows.description: Pd Mr Halifax his Bill
rows.notes: Amount 7 | 2 | 6 legible. Surname 'Halifax' read from script; plausible but with small uncertainty in the initial letter flourish.

rows.description: Pd Mr Bowker for turning & L. Kent
rows.notes: Amount 1 | 2 | 6 readable. Description is highly uncertain: surname appears to be 'Bowker' but the remainder of the line ('turning & L. Kent') is conjectural due to cramped/faded handwriting and unusual letter forms.

rows.description: Pd the Cook for Sewell to wine
rows.notes: Amount 11 | 10 | 0 clear and well aligned. Description reads clearly as payment to the cook 'for Sewell to wine'.

rows.description: Pd for two Bury Books
rows.notes: Amount 2 | 5 | 0 clearly visible. Description 'two Bury Books' legible; minimal ambiguity.
